<!-- Generated by dart run tool/visibility/generate_visibility.dart. Edit tool/visibility/visibility_manifest.json instead. -->
# pqforge

> Post-quantum encryption, signing, hybrid sessions, gigabyte-scale streaming, and a CLI for Dart, Flutter, and Serverpod — built on pqcrypto ML-KEM/ML-DSA.
> Version 0.2.2; MIT licensed.

pqforge turns the pqcrypto ML-KEM (FIPS 203) and ML-DSA (FIPS 204) primitives into application-ready cryptography: KEM-DEM envelopes, AES-256-GCM and ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD, X25519/Ed25519/ECDSA-P256 hybrids, bounded-memory gigabyte streaming, multi-recipient envelopes, Argon2id-wrapped key custody, named recipes, and a universal CLI. The core is pure Dart and web-safe — no dart:ffi.

## Start Here

- Website: https://turkananation.github.io/pqforge/
- Repository: https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge
- Package: https://pub.dev/packages/pqforge
- API docs: https://pub.dev/documentation/pqforge/latest/
- Wiki: https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/wiki
- Built on pqcrypto: https://pub.dev/packages/pqcrypto

## Built On pqcrypto

pqforge is the application layer built on top of pqcrypto. pqcrypto provides the post-quantum primitives; pqforge provides everything you need to ship a feature with them.

- pqcrypto (primitives): pqcrypto is the primitives package: pure-Dart NIST FIPS 203 ML-KEM and FIPS 204 ML-DSA, plus SHA-2 and SHA-3/SHAKE, with zero runtime dependencies. It exposes raw key generation, encapsulation/decapsulation, and sign/verify over byte arrays.
- pqforge (composition): pqforge is the composition package: KEM-DEM envelopes, AES-256-GCM/ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD, X25519/Ed25519/ECDSA-P256 hybrids, bounded-memory streaming, multi-recipient envelopes, Argon2id key custody, named recipes (files, folders, text, media, email, documents, webhooks, tokens, records, logs, artifacts), and a CLI.
- pqcrypto package: https://pub.dev/packages/pqcrypto
- pqcrypto repository: https://github.com/turkananation/pqcrypto

## Key Boundary

- pqforge composes pqcrypto ML-KEM/ML-DSA with AEAD, KDF, custody, recipes, and CLI workflows.
- It is not a validated cryptographic module.
- It does not provide:
  - CMVP/FIPS 140 module validation
  - A hard constant-time guarantee for Dart runtimes
  - A hard memory-erasure guarantee
  - Public-key trust, identity vetting, or legal e-signature policy
  - Replay stores, authorization policy, TLS, or secure infrastructure operations
  - RC4 or unauthenticated encryption

## Never Say

- FIPS validated
- FIPS 140 validated
- CMVP validated
- certified
- hard constant-time Dart guarantee
- hard memory-erasure guarantee
- ML-KEM alone is secure transport
- AES signs documents
- RC4 is supported

## Recipes

- Local File Vault: encryptFileBytes, decryptFileBytes, encryptAsync, PqEnvelope (developer machines, backup jobs, admin laptops, server batch jobs).
- Folder Archive: encryptFolderEntry, decryptFolderEntry (case files, release bundles, evidence folders, internal archives).
- Folder Pack Archive: PqForgePackService (package:pqforge/pqforge_io.dart) (many small files, site exports, evidence bundles, dataset shards).
- Large Files And Media Streaming: PqForgeStreamCipher.encryptFile, decryptStream (package:pqforge/pqforge_io.dart) (gigabyte media, datasets, disk images, backups, VM snapshots).
- Multi-Recipient Envelopes: encryptAsync, PqMultiRecipient, PqRecipientSpec (shared vaults, team archives, escrow, break-glass recipients).
- Text Snippets: sealText, openText, signText, verifyText (notes, secrets, prompts, policy snippets, short messages).
- Media Assets: sealMedia, openMedia, signMedia, verifyMedia (photos, audio, video, PDFs, campaign media, identity images).
- Document Signing: signDocument, verifyDocument (contracts, reports, certificates, approvals, policy documents).
- Hybrid And Classical Signatures: PqForgeHybridSigner, PqEcdsaP256, dualSign, dualVerify (release artifacts, firmware, long-lived approvals, dual-trust signing).
- Webhook Integrity: signWebhook, verifyWebhook (payment callbacks, audit delivery, server-to-server events).
- Signed Tokens: issueToken, verifyToken (API capabilities, admin actions, short-lived grants).
- Private Email Payloads: sealEmail, openEmail (encrypted mail bodies, secure notifications, outbound archive mail).
- Government And Medical Records: encryptRecord, appendSignedLogEntry (public-sector records, patient records, registry payloads).
- Software Artifacts: signArtifact, verifyArtifact (release bundles, firmware, packages, configuration rollouts).
- Hybrid Server Sessions: PqForgeHybridKeyAgreement, PqForgeSecureSession (Serverpod, API services, local agents, backend handshakes).

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/README.md): High-signal overview, badges, quickstarts, recipe map, and validation commands.
- [Documentation Index](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/INDEX.md): Canonical map of every doc, the package surface, and the visibility generator.
- [CLI Guide](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/CLI.md): Full command-line usage: wrapped keys, files, folders, text, media, streaming, pack, multi-recipient, hybrid, and signatures.
- [Project Catalog](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/cookbook/PROJECT_CATALOG.md): Applications and domains that map to pqforge recipes.
- [Recipe Cookbook](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/cookbook/README.md): Named recipe docs for safe application composition.
- [API Reference](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/API.md): Facade methods, hybrid tier, secure sessions, AEAD engines, types, and CLI summary.
- [Hybrid Audit](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/HYBRID_AUDIT.md): Coverage audit against pqcrypto building blocks and explicit rejections.
- [Performance And Hybrid CLI](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/technical/PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_AND_HYBRID_CLI.md): As-built throughput, memory, and engine/cipher recommendations with measured numbers.
- [Envelope Formats](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/architecture/ENVELOPE_FORMATS.md): The .pqf one-shot and .pqfs streaming container layouts and AAD binding.
- [Threat Model](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/security/THREAT_MODEL.md): Assets, adversaries, what the package defends, and what the application must own.
- [Key Custody](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/architecture/KEY_CUSTODY.md): Wrapped key storage and pluggable custody model.
- [Claim Boundary](https://github.com/turkananation/pqforge/blob/main/doc/security/CLAIM_BOUNDARY.md): Allowed and forbidden claims for package consumers and agents.
